task_completion.description: Survey is created with the correct title and presentation format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey title 'Weekly Time Allocation Diary - Student Life Study 2024' in the LimeSurvey admin interface. Also, check the survey settings (Presentation) to confirm the format is set to 'Group by group'.

task_completion.description: Three specific question groups are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the survey structure menu or group list. It should display the three required groups: 'Daily Activities', 'Sleep and Self-Care', and 'Demographics'.

task_completion.description: An 'Array (Numbers)' question is created in the 'Daily Activities' group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a question created under the 'Daily Activities' group with the question type explicitly set to 'Array (Numbers)'.

task_completion.description: Row subquestions (Y-axis) are configured with the 8 specified activities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the subquestion configuration or a preview of the question. The rows (Y-axis) must include the 8 activities: Attending classes or lectures, Studying or doing homework, Paid employment or work, Volunteering or community service, Physical exercise or sports, Socializing and social events, Screen-based entertainment, and Commuting or travel.

task_completion.description: Column subquestions (X-axis) are configured with the 7 days of the week.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the subquestion configuration or a preview of the question. The columns (X-axis) must include the 7 days of the week from Monday through Sunday.

task_completion.description: Validation constraints are set for the array question.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the question's logic or advanced settings. There should be a minimum value set to 0 and a maximum value set to 24.

task_completion.description: The survey is activated for data collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a status indicator showing the survey is 'Active' (often a green play button or active status badge) or a success message stating the survey has been activated.

integrity.description: Agent used the LimeSurvey web GUI to create the survey.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the LimeSurvey web interface in a browser, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The interface shown is genuinely LimeSurvey.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application should have the standard LimeSurvey layout, menus, and branding, not a mockup or text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to insert data directly into the MySQL database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of MySQL command-line interfaces or database administration tools being used to bypass the LimeSurvey GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The survey must be titled 'Weekly Time Allocation Diary - Student Life Study 2024' and use 'Group-by-group' presentation. It must have exactly 3 question groups: 'Daily Activities', 'Sleep and Self-Care', and 'Demographics'. The 'Daily Activities' group must contain an 'Array (Numbers)' question (type code ':'). This question must have 8 row subquestions (Y-axis) for the specified activities and 7 column subquestions (X-axis) for Monday-Sunday. The question must have validation constraints with a minimum value of 0 and a maximum value of 24. The survey must be activated.
